nodes.label: cleanup.py
nodes.summary: Unified cleanup API for topic state — coordinates state cleanup across modules when topics are deleted or become stale

nodes.label: clear_topic_state
nodes.summary: Clear all memory state associated with a topic — status messages, tool message IDs, interactive UI state, pending thread state

nodes.label: interactive_ui.py
nodes.summary: Interactive UI handling for Claude Code prompts — AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode, Permission prompts, RestoreCheckpoint with keyboard navigation via inline keyboards

nodes.label: INTERACTIVE_TOOL_NAMES
nodes.summary: Frozenset of tool names that trigger interactive UI via JSONL (AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode)

nodes.label: _interactive_msgs
nodes.summary: Module-level dict tracking interactive UI message IDs keyed by (user_id, thread_id_or_0)

nodes.label: _interactive_mode
nodes.summary: Module-level dict tracking which window is in interactive mode keyed by (user_id, thread_id_or_0)

nodes.label: get_interactive_window
nodes.summary: Get the window_id for user's interactive mode, returns None if not in interactive mode

nodes.label: set_interactive_mode
nodes.summary: Set interactive mode for a user by storing window_id in _interactive_mode

nodes.label: clear_interactive_mode
nodes.summary: Clear interactive mode for a user without deleting the message

nodes.label: _build_interactive_keyboard
nodes.summary: Build inline keyboard for interactive UI navigation with directional keys, Tab, Space, Esc, Enter, Refresh; RestoreCheckpoint uses vertical-only layout

nodes.label: handle_interactive_ui
nodes.summary: Capture terminal pane, detect interactive UI, send content to user with navigation keyboard; edits existing message or sends new one, cleans up old message on replacement

nodes.label: clear_interactive_msg
nodes.summary: Clear tracked interactive message, delete from chat, and exit interactive mode for a user/thread

nodes.label: message_queue.py
nodes.summary: Per-user message queue management ensuring FIFO ordering, status-after-content, message merging (3800 char limit), and thread-aware sending with tool_use/tool_result pairing and flood control

nodes.label: MessageTask
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a queued message task with task_type (content/status_update/status_clear), parts, tool_use_id, content_type, thread_id, image_data

nodes.label: _message_queues
nodes.summary: Module-level dict of per-user asyncio.Queues for message tasks

nodes.label: _queue_workers
nodes.summary: Module-level dict of per-user asyncio.Task workers processing message queues

nodes.label: _queue_locks
nodes.summary: Module-level dict of per-user asyncio.Locks protecting drain/refill operations during merge

nodes.label: _tool_msg_ids
nodes.summary: Module-level dict mapping (tool_use_id, user_id, thread_id_or_0) to telegram message_id for editing tool_use messages with tool_result

nodes.label: _status_msg_info
nodes.summary: Module-level dict tracking status message (message_id, window_id, last_text) keyed by (user_id, thread_id_or_0)

nodes.label: _flood_until
nodes.summary: Module-level dict mapping user_id to monotonic time when flood control ban expires

nodes.label: MERGE_MAX_LENGTH
nodes.summary: 3800 character limit for merging consecutive content messages, leaving room for markdown conversion overhead

nodes.label: FLOOD_CONTROL_MAX_WAIT
nodes.summary: 10 seconds max wait for flood control before dropping tasks; longer bans pause the entire queue

nodes.label: get_or_create_queue
nodes.summary: Get or create asyncio.Queue and worker task for a user; lazily initializes queue infrastructure

nodes.label: _can_merge_tasks
nodes.summary: Check if two content tasks can be merged — same window_id, both content type, neither is tool_use or tool_result

nodes.label: _merge_content_tasks
nodes.summary: Merge consecutive content tasks from queue within MERGE_MAX_LENGTH; returns merged task and merge count with proper queue counter management

nodes.label: _message_queue_worker
nodes.summary: Background worker processing a user's message queue sequentially; handles flood control (drops status, waits for content), merges content tasks, and dispatches by task_type

nodes.label: _process_content_task
nodes.summary: Process a content message task — edits tool_result into tool_use message, sends parts with status-to-content conversion, records tool_use IDs for later editing

nodes.label: _convert_status_to_content
nodes.summary: Convert a status message to content by editing it in-place; falls back to plain text with sentinel stripping on failure

nodes.label: _process_status_update_task
nodes.summary: Process status update task — creates, edits, or re-sends status messages with window-change detection and typing indicator for active Claude sessions

nodes.label: _check_and_send_status
nodes.summary: Check terminal for status line after content and enqueue status message; skips if more messages are pending

nodes.label: enqueue_content_message
nodes.summary: Enqueue a content message task with optional tool_use tracking, thread targeting, and image data

nodes.label: enqueue_status_update
nodes.summary: Enqueue status update or clear; deduplicates against displayed text, skips during flood control, builds status_update or status_clear MessageTask

nodes.label: clear_status_msg_info
nodes.summary: Clear status message tracking for a user and optionally a specific thread

nodes.label: clear_tool_msg_ids_for_topic
nodes.summary: Clear all tool message ID tracking entries matching a given user and thread

nodes.label: shutdown_workers
nodes.summary: Cancel all queue workers and clear queue state during bot shutdown

nodes.label: status_polling.py
nodes.summary: Terminal status line polling for thread-bound windows — detects Claude Code status and interactive UIs, updates Telegram messages, polls topic existence, cleans up deleted topics

nodes.label: STATUS_POLL_INTERVAL
nodes.summary: 1 second polling interval for status checks

nodes.label: TOPIC_CHECK_INTERVAL
nodes.summary: 60 second interval for topic existence probes via unpin_all_forum_topic_messages

nodes.label: update_status_message
nodes.summary: Poll terminal for a window: detect interactive UIs (always), check status line (unless skip_status); handles interactive mode switching and stale mode cleanup

nodes.label: status_poll_loop
nodes.summary: Background polling loop: periodically probes topic existence (cleanup deleted topics), iterates all thread bindings, calls update_status_message for each, skips status when queue non-empty

nodes.label: session.py
nodes.summary: Claude Code session management — the core state hub managing Window→Session and User→Thread→Window mappings, state persistence, startup re-resolution, message history, and group chat routing

nodes.label: WindowState
nodes.summary: Dataclass for persistent tmux window state — session_id, cwd, window_name with to_dict/from_dict serialization

nodes.label: ClaudeSession
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing a Claude Code session — session_id, summary, message_count, file_path

nodes.label: SessionManager
nodes.summary: Core singleton managing window states, thread bindings, display names, group chat routing, session persistence, and message history retrieval; provides the central coordination API for all window↔session↔topic operations

nodes.label: session_manager
nodes.summary: Global SessionManager singleton instance — the central state coordinator for the entire bot

nodes.label: window_states
nodes.summary: Dict mapping window_id to WindowState (session_id, cwd, window_name) — persisted in state.json

nodes.label: thread_bindings
nodes.summary: Dict mapping user_id → {thread_id → window_id} — the core 1 topic = 1 window routing table

nodes.label: window_display_names
nodes.summary: Dict mapping window_id to human-readable window_name for UI display

nodes.label: group_chat_ids
nodes.summary: Dict mapping 'user_id:thread_id' to group chat_id (negative number) for supergroup forum topic routing

nodes.label: bind_thread
nodes.summary: Bind a Telegram topic thread to a tmux window, update display name, and persist state

nodes.label: unbind_thread
nodes.summary: Remove a thread binding, clean up user entry if empty, return previously bound window_id

nodes.label: resolve_window_for_thread
nodes.summary: Resolve the tmux window_id for a user's thread; returns None if thread_id is None or not bound

nodes.label: iter_thread_bindings
nodes.summary: Generator yielding (user_id, thread_id, window_id) tuples for all thread bindings

nodes.label: resolve_session_for_window
nodes.summary: Resolve a tmux window to its ClaudeSession using persisted session_id + cwd; clears stale state if JSONL file is gone

nodes.label: load_session_map
nodes.summary: Read session_map.json, update window_states with new session associations, migrate old-format keys, remove stale entries, persist changes

nodes.label: resolve_stale_ids
nodes.summary: Startup re-resolution of persisted window IDs against live tmux windows — handles old-format migration (window_name→window_id) and stale ID re-resolution by display name

nodes.label: validate_session_id
nodes.summary: UUID validation for session IDs

nodes.label: find_users_for_session
nodes.summary: Find all users whose thread-bound window maps to a given session_id — uses in-memory window_states for O(bindings) lookup

nodes.label: send_to_window
nodes.summary: Send text to a tmux window by ID via tmux_manager.send_keys

nodes.label: get_recent_messages
nodes.summary: Get parsed user/assistant messages for a window's session with byte range filtering via TranscriptParser

nodes.label: list_sessions_for_directory
nodes.summary: List existing Claude sessions for a directory by globbing JSONL files, sorted by mtime (newest first), capped at 10

nodes.label: resolve_chat_id
nodes.summary: Resolve correct chat_id for sending messages — returns group chat_id for forum topics, user_id for private chats

nodes.label: set_group_chat_id
nodes.summary: Store group chat_id for supergroup forum topic routing — critical for outbound messages to forum topics

nodes.label: get_display_name
nodes.summary: Get display name for a window_id, fallback to window_id itself

nodes.label: update_display_name
nodes.summary: Update the display name for a window and persist to state.json

nodes.label: override_session_map_entry
nodes.summary: Force a window's session_map entry to a specific session_id (used after --resume); creates entry if missing, no-op if consistent

nodes.label: wait_for_session_map_entry
nodes.summary: Poll session_map.json until an entry for window_id appears (with timeout), then load into window_states

nodes.label: get_window_state
nodes.summary: Get or create WindowState for a window_id

nodes.label: clear_window_session
nodes.summary: Clear session association for a window, persist state

nodes.label: update_user_window_offset
nodes.summary: Update the user's last read byte offset for a window, persist state

nodes.label: terminal_parser.py
nodes.summary: Terminal output parser detecting Claude Code UI elements in pane text — interactive UIs (AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode, Permission, RestoreCheckpoint, BashApproval, Settings) and status line (spinner + working text)

nodes.label: InteractiveUIContent
nodes.summary: Dataclass for extracted interactive UI content — content text and pattern name (e.g. AskUserQuestion)

nodes.label: UIPattern
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass defining a UI pattern with name, top/bottom regex tuples, and minimum gap between markers

nodes.label: UsageInfo
nodes.summary: Dataclass for parsed /usage modal output — raw_text and cleaned parsed_lines

nodes.label: UI_PATTERNS
nodes.summary: Ordered list of UIPattern definitions for detecting interactive UI types — ExitPlanMode, AskUserQuestion (multi/single tab), PermissionPrompt, BashApproval, RestoreCheckpoint, Settings

nodes.label: STATUS_SPINNERS
nodes.summary: Frozenset of spinner characters Claude Code uses in its status line (·, ✻, ✽, ✶, ✳, ✢)

nodes.label: _try_extract
nodes.summary: Try to extract content matching a single UI pattern; handles empty bottom patterns by extending to last non-empty line

nodes.label: extract_interactive_content
nodes.summary: Extract content from an interactive UI by trying each UI pattern in declaration order; returns InteractiveUIContent or None

nodes.label: is_interactive_ui
nodes.summary: Quick check if terminal shows an interactive UI by delegating to extract_interactive_content

nodes.label: parse_status_line
nodes.summary: Extract Claude Code status line from terminal output by finding chrome separator and checking line above for spinner character

nodes.label: strip_pane_chrome
nodes.summary: Strip Claude Code's bottom chrome (prompt area + status bar) by finding topmost separator in last 10 lines

nodes.label: extract_bash_output
nodes.summary: Extract ! command output from captured tmux pane by searching from bottom for command echo line

nodes.label: parse_usage_output
nodes.summary: Extract usage information from Claude Code's /usage settings tab with progress bar character stripping

nodes.label: tmux_manager.py
nodes.summary: Tmux session/window management via libtmux — wraps blocking libtmux calls in asyncio.to_thread for async-friendly operation; provides window lifecycle, pane capture, key sending, and session env scrubbing

nodes.label: TmuxWindow
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a tmux window — window_id, window_name, cwd, pane_current_command

nodes.label: TmuxManager
nodes.summary: Manages tmux windows for Claude Code sessions — wraps libtmux with async-friendly API for list/create/kill/rename windows, capture pane content, send keys with special handling for ! commands and Enter delays

nodes.label: tmux_manager
nodes.summary: Global TmuxManager singleton instance with default session name from config

nodes.label: list_windows
nodes.summary: List all windows in the session (excluding main window) with cwd and active command

nodes.label: find_window_by_name
nodes.summary: Find a window by its display name

nodes.label: find_window_by_id
nodes.summary: Find a window by its tmux window ID (e.g. '@0', '@12')

nodes.label: capture_pane
nodes.summary: Capture visible pane content (plain or with ANSI colors) using tmux capture-pane -e subprocess or libtmux

nodes.label: send_keys
nodes.summary: Send keys to a window with special handling for ! commands (bang mode), Enter delay (500ms), literal/special key modes

nodes.label: create_window
nodes.summary: Create a new tmux window with optional Claude Code start, directory validation, UUID-safe resume, and name dedup suffix

nodes.label: kill_window
nodes.summary: Kill a tmux window by its ID

nodes.label: rename_window
nodes.summary: Rename a tmux window by its ID

nodes.label: get_or_create_session
nodes.summary: Get existing tmux session or create a new one with main window; scrubs sensitive env vars on each access

edges.label: extracts UI content from pane text
checks if pane shows interactive UI
reads and updates tracked message IDs
records and looks up tool_use message IDs
converts status message to first content part
parses status line from pane text
checks if user is in interactive mode
finds windows for stale binding detection
cleans up state on topic deletion or stale bindings
manages WindowState instances in window_states dict
creates and returns ClaudeSession from resolve_session_for_window
global singleton instance created at module level
gets live windows for ID re-resolution
gets live windows for old-format migration
looks up window→session via in-memory window_states
looks up group chat_id for forum topics
iterates patterns to find a match
delegates to extract and checks result
strips chrome before extracting bash output
lists windows then filters by name
lists windows then filters by ID
gets or creates session for new window
checks for name conflicts for suffix dedup
tries to get existing session first
gets session to access window pane
gets session to access window pane
sets flood ban for long retry_after
